description: Create the sprite generation system (SpriteGenerator.java) and animation state machine (SpriteAnimator.java, AnimationState.java). Generate all entity sprites (Player, Goblin, PlagueGoblin, Skeleton, ShieldedSkeleton, GoblinKing, GoblinChieftain, BoneColossus, Lich) with animation frames (IDLE 2 frames, ATTACK 2-3 frames, HURT 1-2 frames, DEATH 2-3 frames). Also generate all UI sprites: HP/MP bar frames, status effect icons (Poison, Shield, Stun, Regen, Enrage, Curse), chest sprites (3 frames), menu frame border, wave banner, element icons, background tiles, and particle effect textures. Then create a ColorPalette.java utility with the specified NES-style 16-32 color palette constants.
steps: Create src/view/sprites/ColorPalette.java with all the hex color constants specified in the requirements (background #0D0D1A, dungeon walls #2A2A3A, player steel blue #4A6FA5, goblins forest green #2D8B2D, skeletons bone white #E8E8D0, lich deep purple #4A0080, fire orange #FF6600, holy gold #FFD700, poison toxic green #00CC44, dark purple #8B00FF, HP red #CC0000, MP blue #0066CC, UI borders warm gray #8B8B7A, text off-white #E8E8E8, damage text red #FF3333, heal text green #33FF33, crit text yellow #FFFF00, etc.)
Create src/view/sprites/AnimationState.java as an enum with values: IDLE, ATTACKING, HURT, DYING, CASTING, DEAD

Create src/view/sprites/SpriteGenerator.java using Pixmap API to draw authentic 8-bit pixel art. Each entity sprite should use deliberate pixel-by-pixel placement to look like retro art, not just colored rectangles. Generate at 16x16, 24x24, or 32x32 base resolution. Methods: generatePlayerSprite(), generateGoblinSprite(), generatePlagueGoblinSprite(), generateSkeletonSprite(), generateShieldedSkeletonSprite(), generateGoblinKingSprite(), generateGoblinChieftainSprite(), generateBoneColossusSprite(), generateLichSprite(). Each returns TextureRegion[] arrays for each animation state. Also: generateHpBarFrame(), generateMpBarFrame(), generateStatusIcons(), generateChestSprites(), generateMenuFrame(), generateWaveBanner(), generateElementIcons(), generateBackgroundTile(), generateParticleTextures(). All textures use TextureFilter.Nearest.

Create src/view/sprites/SpriteAnimator.java that manages animation state per entity. Fields: currentState (AnimationState), stateTime (float), frames (Map<AnimationState, TextureRegion[]>), frameDurations (Map<AnimationState, Float>). Methods: setState(AnimationState), update(float delta), getCurrentFrame() -> TextureRegion, isFinished() -> boolean. Idle loops at 0.15s/frame, attack at 0.1s/frame, hurt plays once then returns to IDLE, death plays once then stays on last frame (DEAD state).
acceptance_criteria: SpriteGenerator creates authentic pixel art using Pixmap (not plain colored rectangles) for all 9 entity types
Each entity has frames for IDLE (2), ATTACKING (2-3), HURT (1-2), DEATH (2-3)
SpriteAnimator correctly transitions between states, loops IDLE, plays attack/hurt/death once
All UI sprites are generated: HP/MP bar frames, 6 status icons, 3 chest frames, menu frame, wave banner, 5 element icons, background tile, particle textures
ColorPalette provides all specified color constants
verification: Run ./gradlew classes and confirm BUILD SUCCESSFUL with no errors
